extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo dominated the flat 26km ITT by 1:41 over BISSEGGER Stefan, while KUSS Sepp unexpectedly retained red by 40 seconds over SOLER Marc through exceptional pacing discipline.
tactical_patterns: GANNA caught four riders starting from his 1-minute man, indicating extreme power differential that forced pure climbers to recalibrate ITT expectations.
KUSS executed all-in pacing strategy on dead-flat ITT despite climber profile, suggesting UAE/Soudal misjudged his true TT ceiling by 40+ seconds.
EVENEPOEL finished only 16 seconds faster than KUSS, revealing critical weakness in converting flat TT superiority against motivated pure climbers.
Pure climbers AYUSO and MARTINEZ cracked top-10 ITT results, signaling new generation climbers prioritize TT development unlike previous cohorts.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Retained red by 40s over SOLER on dead-flat ITT far outside wheelhouse, complicates Jumbo-Visma leadership dynamics for mountain stages.
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 1:41 margin over BISSEGGER at 53kph average shows track-honed team pursuit empty-tank ability translates to road ITT dominance.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Only 16 seconds faster than KUSS reveals inability to maximize advantages on terrain designed for his skillset, questionable for GC pressure.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: 1:36 down on KUSS and 20 seconds slower than EVENEPOEL indicates either form deficit or deliberate domestique role acceptance.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: Lost red by 26 seconds suggests conservative pacing or misjudged effort distribution on straightforward flat course.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Seventh place among pure climbers most impressive, signals complete rider development trajectory unlike traditional single-dimension climbers.
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: neutral: European champion beaten by 1:41 establishes GANNA as clear tier-one ITT reference, but BISSEGGER remains elite baseline.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ William: positive: Moved into top-10 GC via ITT, indicating emerging climbers now arrive with sufficient chrono skills to defend GC positions.
